a range of indemnity private medical treatment products for individuals

last few years both profits and volumes of in-force business has been significantly lower

several competitors have entered the pmi market
selling pmi products more cheaply

currently sells through direct sales force, brokers and telephone sales

solvency position has deteriorated noticeably

two suggestions: 
discount to prem based on loyalty
start selling products through the internet

=====================

low volumes could be due to - low new business or low renewals or both

general reasons applicable to both:
    economic recession so lower sales overall
    more employers offering group schemes
     removal of tax incentives
     improvement in state healthcare

low new business:
reduction in market
    adverse publicity of products
reduction in the company's share
    ineffective marketing and sales
    brand image is tarnished
    hospital networks not extensively available
    reduction in quality of service in the hospitals
competitor actions
    selling more attractive products
          if they are selling as loss leaders, they may not be able to sustain it for long
    using latest sales methods like internet and social media
          this will sustain
    pricing more attractively
    targeting younger population so they are able to price economically
           the initial selection effect will wear off eventually and they will have to reprice or exit the market
Distributor actions
    not selling effectively
    brokers advocating another insurer product
  
low renewals:
   product not meeting the customer needs
   poor service and claims settlement experience

sa1_e_notes_Ch00_0.2_null:
nbs occurs when

initial asset share (prem recd less initial expenses)
less than supervisory reserves and solvency capital reqt

any such strain has to be made good from co's free assets

(a) CI - for regular prem contracts asset share is negative in early years
because of high level of expenses - commission, sales mktng, u/w, pol documentation
and these are likely to exceed the amt of first prem
reserves may need to be atleast zero due to supervisory restrns + sol capital may be reqd
thus liab exceeds assets and there is nbs

(b) ltci - common to have nbs despite large positive net cash inflow ie the single prem at time zero
this arises due to valn basis being stronger than the prem basis
and/or due to solvency capital reqt

sa1_e_notes_Ch00_0.1_(ii):
in order to offer regular level premiums for  a long term contract, the insurer must be able to assess the risk, in a statistical sense
this is hard because claim size unlikely to remain same over time
this may be due to changes in - med inflation, kinds of ailments, regimes used for treatment
as one ailment becomes treatable in cheap and efficient way, others become prevalent
costs of these emerging treatment regimes are difficult to predict, so difficult to predict long term costs
distrbn of estimates will have large variance, so large contingency loadings reqd
resulting prem levels too high to be acceptable

sa1_e_ans_Ch28_28.9_(ii):
the spread b/w the risk free bonds and the risky bonds will have two components
one is the premium for the default risk 
other is for the lack of marketability of the bond

the second one is called the illiquidity premium

if the assets are matched to the liabilities in term, then there is no need to liquidate the bonds mid term
so the illiquidity premium is a genuine gain to the insurer

so regulator sometimes permits adding this illiquidity premium to the risk free rate which is used to discount the liab
this will lead to lower liabilities
however certain conditions are to be met to be eligible for this concession

liquidity plan 
matching of the assets with the liab duration

if equity has been used for mismatch and higher returns then property also should provide long term real returns

expertise

can be unmarketable

Indirect property will be more suitable if property exposure is desired

sa1_e_notes_Ch28_28.4_null:
matching means - equal and opposite asset to liab

this is impossible in practice

so looser sense - same nature, term and currency

matching reduces market exposure - eg int rates, eq movements
however removes possibility of profit

if free assets are low, only write business that can be matched
if free assets are large, then mismatch in hope of making a profit

extensive matching unlikely on health prods as uncertainty of outgo on protection prods
eg CI - term matching not possible

however where matching is achievable, prems can be priced competitively

matching impossible for an expanding fund as no assets avbl w/ initially negative income or suff long term

a lesser protection is immunization - even then liab so long that there are no suitable assets

The company has therefore decided to investigate the situation more fully, including an assessment of the financial consequences for the company if the situation were to persist.

sa1_e_notes_Ch28_28.8_null:
start by mentioning the principles of investment
match nature term currency / maximize returns / depart to the extent within risk appetite and free assets
 
matching pf ltci
term - long, so long term assets / but will depend on incidence and termination rates
nature - 
level or incr at known rate ie guar in money terms - then fi bonds
incr in line w/ a specified index - then securities linked to same index if available
cover the costs of care ie indemnity - then real return such as equities
be based on the value of a fund ie unit linked - then invst in same assets
for indem based w/ cap - mix of il bonds and real assets like equity based on likely cap biting

allow for future premiums - known at outset but may be reviewable
these can be considered as negative benefit payments

perfect matching is impossible in practice 
difficult to find assets of long enough duration


matching group ip
written over one or two year term - so only consider sickness claims that arise over this period
consider the length of the claim payments
most claimants likely to recover within short time eg a year
for these cases invst return not sig - so mix of cash and short term fi bonds
however some claims of longer duration eg lasting till ret age
consider whether these increase in payment - if so fixed or index linked - match w/ long term fi or il bonds

Other considerations:
match w/ same currency
consider expense liability - this tends to increase over time - probably an earnings index 
suitable match is bonds linked to this index if available - if not avbl then real assets like eq or property

high level of free assets means can depart from above matching - in hope of high returns
therefore higher proportion of high yielding assets like eq / prop
however high free assets required to expand business - so this will limit mismatching

consider the cos risk appetite - prob of ruin

asset liab matching techniques can be used - to assess impact

consider regulatory constraints - whether or how much particular asset can be held, admissible assets, mandatory assets eg gilts, mismatching reserve red free assets, extent of diversification

hold enough liquid and marketable assets to meet unexp payments - or to take adv of opportunities
for expanding co this will not be an issue as prem inc likely exceed outgo

consider deferred period on the products - will give insurer notice

consider shareholder appetite for risk

consider diff in tax efficiency

investment strategy to be in line with that of competitors
any publicly stated intentions must be honoured

sa1_e_ans_SA1PQ02_2.1_(i):
standalone critical insurance (saci) vs income protection insurance (ip)
risks covered and benefits available

saci covers the risk of suffering from any of the major illnesses specified in the policy
eg stroke, heart attack, etc
there will be a survival period usually 30 days

ip covers the risk of being unable to work due to sickness or injury
there will be a deferred period after which payments start

saci pays a lump sum benefit
ip pays a regular income

for saci the benefits could be tiered becoming payable in stages depending on the  seriousness of the illness
ip benefits may be level or increasing
the increase may be during payment or otherwise or both

Valuation principles and methods under Solvency II for -
Assets
Liabilities
Specific provisions related to P&L volatility

Assets:
three components - investments, reinsurance recoveries, participations (>20% holdings) 
Prudent person principle
market values of assets
invested to best match the liabilities by nature term and currency
derivatives only to be used for risk management

Liabilities:
BEL
best estimate assmns
till contract boundary - usually till prem paying term
all decrements all policy holder actions should be considered
policy by policy but model points may be acceptable in some condns
discount rates is the yield on bonds of matching duration
these rates are published by eiopa
rates must be of the same currency bonds

RM
additional capital held for taking the bel liabilities
it is the provision for variance of the best est liab
cost of capital approach
6% is the current cost of capital rate
this is the capital for non hedgeable market risks and other than market risks
ie insurance risks

P&L volatility:
matching adjustment
adjustment for illiquidity premium
liquidity plan is to be submitted

volatility adjustment
for assets where the matching adj is not available
to safeguard against distress sale of assets during times of market downturn

sa1_e_ans_Ch17_17.1_null:
more hospital capacity will bring down prices of medical services
so will reduce the claim amounts for a pmi policy

medical service progress can have both effects
if procedures become more reliable and easily available more people will claim
if the cost of treatment comes down due to innovation then claims amounts will come down
also advanced treatment might reduce recovery times eg keyhole surgery - this may reduce costs

better deals will reduce costs
also this will act as a check on the treatment provided by the provider

inflation will increase the claim amounts
so will increase claim costs
sa1_e_ans_SP1IFoA202409_1_ii:
approach to price the gio:

two ways - one to assume all will exercise the option - this is too conservative - increase the cost of policy
another to estimate the proportion who will exercise the option - this will require stochastic projections

sa1_e_ans_Ch30_30.1_(i):
Whether mortality assmns will change?

Mortality of ph in higher level of care is expected to be higher than those in lower level of care
The initial condition is that the ph in lower level of care will continue in that level and cannot be changed
So the mortality assmn in pricing has to take account of the prob that the ph could move to the higher level of care during the term of the policy
and thereby the mortality assmn will have to adjusted for that level
